summary: DHH shares cost data from 37signals' company meet-ups, revealing that European locations cost roughly half as much as US ones per person. Amsterdam came in at $3,500 per person compared to $6,200 in Miami and $5,700 in New Orleans, with comparable or better quality. He argues that the savings from eliminating their Chicago office ($25,000/month) more than offset meet-up costs, and strongly recommends remote companies invest in regular in-person gatherings for the human connection they provide.
key_points: European meet-ups cost nearly half of US ones: Amsterdam was $3,500/person vs Miami's $6,200 and New Orleans' $5,700
Transportation costs in the US were a major factor, with Miami cabs and Ubers alone exceeding $10,000 vs a fraction of that for Amsterdam public transit
Eliminating their Chicago office lease ($25,000/month used by a handful of people) more than offsets the cost of biannual meet-ups
Despite 37signals being split 50/50 between US and international employees, staying in the US for meet-ups didn't save on travel costs
The human connection value of in-person meet-ups for remote teams far exceeds their cost
money_quotes: Would you have guessed that hosting a company meet-up in US was almost twice as expensive as Europe? I wouldn't!
I feel like Americans often complain about how expensive things are in Europe, but looking at our expenditures on meet-ups, that just isn't so.
In Miami, we spent well over $10,000 just on cabs and Ubers. Whereas in Amsterdam, the cost was a fraction for the public transit options.
We used to spend a good $25,000/month on the Chicago lease and associated bills. That was an office used by less than a handful of people on a regular basis!
These meet-ups leave such a lasting impression on everyone who participates. It's a super dose of human connection, combined with a fun trip, and some excellent eating.
If you're running a remote company, especially an internationally remote one, I strongly recommend finding a way to fit meet-ups into the budget.
key_insight: Remote companies can get better value hosting team meet-ups in Europe than the US, and the savings from eliminating office leases make regular in-person gatherings an easy investment in human connection.
